{{Quote|Qwi, you were always so brilliant—but in other ways you managed to be incredibly dense.|Bevel Lemelisk|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
'''Qwi Xux''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Omwati/Legends|Omwati]] weapons designer who was responsible due to mental conditioning for aiding in the creation of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], the [[Sun Crusher]], and other important [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]] for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
When she was ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old, Qwi was taken from her village on [[Omwat/Legends|Omwat]], along with nine other children from nine separate villages, upon orders from [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]. Tarkin believed the theory of the massive potential of the Omwati mind and was convinced that, with the proper manipulation, it could wield outstanding mental capacities.

On board a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] Omwat, the ten children underwent rigorous education, mental challenges and testing. If any of the children failed in their testing, they were forced to watch as their entire village was bombarded from orbit, followed by their [[Death/Legends|execution]]. This obviously had a profound psychological effect on the Omwati subjects, warping their view on life and on the challenges they face. This was reflected in Qwi Xux's drive to complete problems—even years later, she greatly feared failure for the prospective repercussions it could bring. Only Qwi survived this [[Torture/Legends|torturous]] captivity.<ref name="Jedi Search" />

After two years of this mental conditioning, she had developed a distorted sense of reality. She maintained that problems had to be solved for their own sake, and convinced herself that the results of her experiments were not her responsibility. Qwi's individual training had focused on forgoing biological knowledge to develop a mind with an unparalleled grasp of physics, mathematics and engineering. She also learned [[Artificial intelligence/Legends|AI]] principles from [[Nasdra Magrody]].

===The Maw Installation===
{{Quote|Good. Now create an even more powerful weapon.|Tarkin to the Maw scientists after the completed development of the Death Star|<ref name="Jedi Search" />}}
[[File:Qwi.jpg|thumb|left|120px|A full body shot of Qwi Xux.]]

Tarkin took Qwi Xux to his highly secret [[Maw Installation]] to develop new weapons systems for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. She begin solving the intricate problems provided to her, working under [[Bevel Lemelisk]], to develop most of the Death Star. However, due to the conditioning she believed that her creations were purely innocent rather than the destructive behemoths they became. The [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], she thought, was developed to break up dead, lifeless [[Planet/Legends|planets]] to extract the valuable ores and elements within. She also thought the same of the [[World Devastator]]s. She also assisted in developing the [[Sun Crusher]]'s [[Nova/Legends|supernova]]-inducing weaponry and [[Quantum-crystalline armor|quantum-crystalline]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]].

It was not until [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] that her fallacy was broken. When [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Kyp Durron]] accidentally discovered the Maw Installation, they were all rigorously and brutally interrogated to discover what had happened to the Empire. [[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] Xux was given the results of these interrogations, which she accepted as simply acquired facts. However, eager for a first-hand account of the Death Star, she sought a meeting with Han. After hearing the Death Star had been used for the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|Destruction of Alderaan]], and the World Devastators had laid waste to much of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], the seeds of doubt were implanted in her mind. After accessing the records of the interrogations, she was horrified at the methods used to procure the information. The thought of her work being the cause of billions of deaths opened her [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] to see the truth behind the terrors she had created.

===The New Republic===
{{Quote|It's the greatest weapon ever devised, and I've spent eight years of my life designing it. You didn't expect me to leave it here for Admiral Daala, did you?|Qwi Xux on the Sun Crusher, to Han Solo|<ref name="Jedi Search" />}}
Realizing the scope of the projects being developed at the Installation, Qwi helped Han, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Kyp Durron]] escape in the [[Sun Crusher]]. Despite being followed by Daala's fleet of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]], they encountered the Kessel mercenary force just outside the Maw and escaped in the ensuing chaos.<ref name="Jedi Search" />

Qwi was put under the protection of [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] as some believed that the information she held would make her a prime candidate for kidnapping. Before they left [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to travel incognito, she urged the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to destroy it before it could have a chance to demonstrate its terrible power. They visited the planet [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]] to help the [[Vor]]s repair the [[Cathedral of Winds/Legends|Cathedral of Winds]]. However, when Qwi used a shard of the tower to play ethereal, haunting music, one of the Vors took the shard from her and crushed it. Fearing they had offended the Vors, the pair left to move undercover.<ref name="Dark Apprentice">[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']]</ref>

Wedge and Qwi became very close during their time together. Wedge took her to [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]], believing she would revel in the wonders of nature of the jungle planet. While there, Qwi Xux was confronted by Kyp Durron, who had abandoned training at [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum]] after falling to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Kyp scoured Qwi's mind, removing any information that could aid in the construction of another superweapon. Wedge nursed her back to health, but she was never the same again.<ref name="Dark Apprentice" />

She and Wedge maintained their relationship, but he was increasingly called upon in military matters, and Qwi became more and more absorbed in her work. Despite a brief stint in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]] as Wedge's science officer, both agreed that the relationship was evolving to be a convenience, rather than a true romance, and they went their separate ways.<ref name="Starfighters of Adumar">''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]''</ref>

Qwi eventually decided to travel to Vortex and help the Vors repair the Cathedral of Winds. While there, she hoped to find her own inner peace and eliminate the memories of all the beings her superweapons had killed.

==Personality and traits==
Qwi Xux underwent much mental conditioning while she was young. As a result, she had a shroud of feigned naïveté for the duration of her life until leaving the Maw Installation. Up until this point, she believed she was not responsible for the results of her experiments or what happened after a problem had been overcome. As such, she could be described as being highly naïve.

She had a passion for [[Music/Legends|music]], often humming or singing while she worked.<ref name="Jedi Search" />

==Behind the scenes==
Qwi Xux was created by author [[Kevin J. Anderson]], based on [[Mermeia]] from ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucasfan.com/swtv/index3.html|text=Star Wars Holiday Special Opinions|work=[https://web.archive.org/web/19981212015351/http://lucasfan.com LucasFan.com]|archivedate=20200120144211|nolive=1}}</ref><ref name="I Have a Bad Feeling About This!">{{InsiderCite|106|I Have a Bad Feeling About This!}}</ref> In the Jedi Academy trilogy, Xux's eyes are given as deep blue, pale blue or violet, whereas in ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' her eyes are clarified as light blue.
